title: Lakshmi Puja
intro: Lakshmi Puja is one of the most sacred Hindu festivals dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi, the deity of wealth, prosperity, fortune, and happiness.
description:
Lakshmi Puja is one of the most sacred Hindu festivals dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi, the deity of wealth, prosperity, fortune, and happiness. It is celebrated across India, but it holds special importance in West Bengal, Odisha, and Eastern India. In Bengal, Lakshmi Puja is mainly celebrated on Kojagari Purnima, while in many other parts of India, Goddess Lakshmi is worshipped on the night of Diwali.

According to Hindu traditions, Goddess Lakshmi emerged during the Samudra Manthan (Churning of the Ocean) and is regarded as the consort of Lord Vishnu. Devotees worship Maa Lakshmi to seek blessings for wealth, success, family happiness, good fortune, and prosperity.

During Kojagari Lakshmi Puja in Bengal, homes are cleaned and decorated, traditional Alpana designs are created, and devotees install the idol or image of Goddess Lakshmi. Lamps are lit, prayers and mantras are chanted, and offerings of flowers, fruits, sweets, and traditional items are made to the Goddess. The festival represents devotion, gratitude, purity, and the welcoming of positive energy into life.

Festival Month

Ashwin / Kartik (September – November)

Significance / Importance

Lakshmi Puja holds great religious and spiritual importance in Hinduism. Goddess Lakshmi is worshipped as the goddess of wealth, prosperity, abundance, fortune, and well-being.

Devotees believe that worshipping Maa Lakshmi with a pure heart brings happiness, peace, financial stability, and positive energy into the home. The festival also teaches the importance of hard work, honesty, cleanliness, gratitude, and devotion.

Puja Vidhi

1. Clean and decorate the home and पूजा area.
2. Install an idol or image of Goddess Lakshmi.
3. Light lamps and invoke Maa Lakshmi with devotion.
4. Offer lotus flowers, fruits, sweets, incense, and Naivedya.
5. Recite Lakshmi Mantras and Shri Sukta.
6. Perform Goddess Lakshmi Aarti.
7. Distribute Prasad and pray for prosperity and happiness.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Samudra Manthan Lakshmi Katha
* Lakshmi Mantra
* Shri Sukta
* Lakshmi Aarti
* Lakshmi Chalisa
* Kanakadhara Stotra

faq.a1: Lakshmi Puja is celebrated on different dates according to regional traditions. In Bengal, it is mainly celebrated on Kojagari Purnima.
faq.a2: Lakshmi Puja is celebrated to seek the blessings of Goddess Lakshmi for wealth, prosperity, happiness, and good fortune.
faq.q3: Where is Lakshmi Puja mainly celebrated?
faq.a3: Lakshmi Puja is celebrated throughout India, with special importance in West Bengal, Odisha, and Eastern India.
faq.a4: Goddess Lakshmi is the Hindu goddess of wealth, prosperity, fortune, and abundance.
faq.q5: What offerings are made during Lakshmi Puja?
faq.a5: Devotees offer lotus flowers, sweets, fruits, lamps, incense, and other traditional offerings to Goddess Lakshmi.
faq.q6: Which prayers are recited during Lakshmi Puja?
faq.a6: Lakshmi Mantras, Shri Sukta, Lakshmi Aarti, and Lakshmi Chalisa are commonly recited.
faq.q7: In which months is Lakshmi Puja celebrated?
faq.a7: Lakshmi Puja is usually celebrated between September and November.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Lakshmi Puja?
faq.a8: Lakshmi Puja symbolizes prosperity, purity, gratitude, devotion, positive energy, and the blessings of Goddess Lakshmi.
